segments.text: In material existence, when a devotee is not completely purified,
he expects material benefit from the Lord in the form of relief from material miseries,
or he wants material gain, advancement in knowledge of the relationship
between the Supreme Personality of Godhead and the living entity,
or knowledge as to the real nature of the Supreme Lord.
When a person is transcendental to these conditions, he is called a pure devotee.
He does not engage himself in the service of the Lord for any material benefit
or for understanding of the Supreme Lord.
His only interest is that he loves that Supreme Personality of Godhead
and he spontaneously engages in satisfying Him.
The highest example of pure devotional service is that of the Gopis in Vrindavan.
They are not interested in understanding Krishna, but only in loving Him.
That platform of love is the pure state of devotional service.
Unless one is advanced to this pure state of devotional service,
there is a tendency to desire elevation to a higher material position.
A mixed devotee may desire to be a pure devotee,
or he may desire to enjoy a comfortable life on another planet
with a greater span of life, such as on Ramaloka.
These are material desires, but because a mixed devotee engages in the service of the Lord,
ultimately, after many, many lives of material enjoyment,
and the symptom of this Krishna consciousness is that he is no longer interested
in any part or any sort of material elevated life.
He does not even aspire to be a pure devotee.
He does not even aspire to become a personality like Lord Brahma.
In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna mentions that the living entities,
the spirit soul, the Atma, is his Amsa.
mam eva amsa jiva loke jiva bhuta sanatana
And Prabhupada explains that how, as Amsa of the Lord,
we are qualitatively the same as that of the Lord.
Prabhupada gives an example of how a drop of water in the sea
has the same characteristics as that of the entire sea,
A drop of water is very minuscule compared to the entire ocean.
So, being Amsa of the Lord,
qualitatively we are one with the Lord and we also are Sat, Chit, and Ananda.
Sat means eternal, Chit means full of knowledge, and Ananda, blissful.
And these Amsas of the Lord, in the spiritual sense, are the same as the Amsa of the Lord.
